revision.comment: 1 revision imported: importing a bunch of english language kofun articles

fixing error that broke all the categories

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Onizuka Kofun | native_name =鬼塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Onizuka Kofun (Kunisaki).jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Onizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Onizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|40|40.0|N|131|34|58.4|E|region:JP-44|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kunisaki, Ōita]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|40|40.0|N|131|34|58.4|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Onizuka Kofun'''|鬼塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in the Kunimi-cho neighborhood of the city of [[Kunisaki, Ōita]], on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211580|title=鬼塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Onizuka Kofun is a slightly elliptical {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳}}-style circular tumulus with a diameter of 10 meters from east-to-west and 13 meters from north-to-south. It is one of the 11 tumuli in the Nishiyama Kofun cluster built on the top of Mt. Ōno, 110 meters above sea level, overlooking Taketatsu Port at the northern tip of the [[Kunisaki Peninsula]]. It has a horizontal stone [[burial chamber]] which is now semi-exposed, and the interior walls of the burial chamber are decorated with line carvings on the back wall of birds, ships, and people on board, on the left wall a flock of birds, and on the right wall two birds facing each other. Since there is a depiction of a person fishing, it can be inferred that the person buried was a powerful person with deep ties to the sea, but there are many later additions and the carvings are shallow, so which lines belong to the original drawing are difficult to trace. It is estimated to have been constructed around the end of the 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
[[Grave goods]] such as iron sword fragments, iron arrowheads, sword fittings, metal rings, glass beads, and [[Sue ware]] have unearthed. Currently, the mound has been restored and the surrounding area has been maintained as a park, with the excavated items are on display at the Kunisaki Furusato Exhibition Hall. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately 40 minutes by car from [[Usa Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/culturalproperties/result/4024/ Japan Heritage home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.kunisaki.oita.jp/soshiki/bunkazai/bunkazainaka.html#a01 Kuniskai City home page]{{in lang|ja}}

The site is approximately 40 minutes by car from [[Usa Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{nihongo|'''Onizuka Kofun'''|鬼塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in the Kunimi-cho neighborhood of the city of [[Kunisaki, Ōita]], on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211580|title=鬼塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Onizuka Kofun is a slightly elliptical {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳}}-style circular tumulus with a diameter of 10 meters from east-to-west and 13 meters from north-to-south. It is one of the 11 tumuli in the Nishiyama Kofun cluster built on the top of Mt. Ōno, 110 meters above sea level, overlooking Taketatsu Port at the northern tip of the [[Kunisaki Peninsula]]. It has a horizontal stone [[burial chamber]] which is now semi-exposed, and the interior walls of the burial chamber are decorated with line carvings on the back wall of birds, ships, and people on board, on the left wall a flock of birds, and on the right wall two birds facing each other. Since there is a depiction of a person fishing, it can be inferred that the person buried was a powerful person with deep ties to the sea, but there are many later additions and the carvings are shallow, so which lines belong to the original drawing are difficult to trace. It is estimated to have been constructed around the end of the 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
[[Grave goods]] such as iron sword fragments, iron arrowheads, sword fittings, metal rings, glass beads, and [[Sue ware]] have unearthed. Currently, the mound has been restored and the surrounding area has been maintained as a park, with the excavated items are on display at the Kunisaki Furusato Exhibition Hall. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately 40 minutes by car from [[Usa Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Onizuka Kofun | native_name =鬼塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Onizuka Kofun (Kunisaki).jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Onizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Onizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|40|40.0|N|131|34|58.4|E|region:JP-44|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kunisaki, Ōita]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|40|40.0|N|131|34|58.4|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Onizuka Kofun'''|鬼塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in the Kunimi-cho neighborhood of the city of [[Kunisaki, Ōita]], on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211580|title=鬼塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Onizuka Kofun is a slightly elliptical {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus with a diameter of 10 meters from east-to-west and 13 meters from north-to-south. It is one of the 11 tumuli in the Nishiyama Kofun cluster built on the top of Mt. Ōno, 110 meters above sea level, overlooking Taketatsu Port at the northern tip of the [[Kunisaki Peninsula]]. It has a horizontal stone [[burial chamber]] which is now semi-exposed, and the interior walls of the burial chamber are decorated with line carvings on the back wall of birds, ships, and people on board, on the left wall a flock of birds, and on the right wall two birds facing each other. Since there is a depiction of a person fishing, it can be inferred that the person buried was a powerful person with deep ties to the sea, but there are many later additions and the carvings are shallow, so which lines belong to the original drawing are difficult to trace. It is estimated to have been constructed around the end of the 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
[[Grave goods]] such as iron sword fragments, iron arrowheads, sword fittings, metal rings, glass beads, and [[Sue ware]] have unearthed. Currently, the mound has been restored and the surrounding area has been maintained as a park, with the excavated items are on display at the Kunisaki Furusato Exhibition Hall. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately 40 minutes by car from [[Usa Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Onizuka Kofun | native_name =鬼塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Onizuka Kofun (Kunisaki).jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Onizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Onizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|40|40.0|N|131|34|58.4|E|region:JP-44|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kunisaki, Ōita]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|40|40.0|N|131|34|58.4|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Onizuka Kofun'''|鬼塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Kunimi-cho neighborhood of the city of [[Kunisaki, Ōita]], on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211580|title=鬼塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Onizuka Kofun is a slightly elliptical {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus with a diameter of 10 meters from east-to-west and 13 meters from north-to-south. It is one of the 11 tumuli in the Nishiyama Kofun cluster built on the top of Mt. Ōno, 110 meters above sea level, overlooking Taketatsu Port at the northern tip of the [[Kunisaki Peninsula]]. It has a horizontal stone [[burial chamber]] which is now semi-exposed, and the interior walls of the burial chamber are decorated with line carvings on the back wall of birds, ships, and people on board, on the left wall a flock of birds, and on the right wall two birds facing each other. Since there is a depiction of a person fishing, it can be inferred that the person buried was a powerful person with deep ties to the sea, but there are many later additions and the carvings are shallow, so which lines belong to the original drawing are difficult to trace. It is estimated to have been constructed around the end of the 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
[[Grave goods]] such as iron sword fragments, iron arrowheads, sword fittings, metal rings, glass beads, and [[Sue ware]] have unearthed. Currently, the mound has been restored and the surrounding area has been maintained as a park, with the excavated items are on display at the Kunisaki Furusato Exhibition Hall. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately 40 minutes by car from [[Usa Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Onizuka Kofun | native_name =鬼塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Onizuka Kofun (Kunisaki).jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Onizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Onizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|40|40.0|N|131|34|58.4|E|region:JP-44|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kunisaki, Ōita]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|40|40.0|N|131|34|58.4|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Onizuka Kofun'''|鬼塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Kunimi-cho neighborhood of the city of [[Kunisaki, Ōita]], on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211580|title=鬼塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Onizuka Kofun is a slightly elliptical {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus with a diameter of 10 meters from east-to-west and 13 meters from north-to-south. It is one of the 11 tumuli in the Nishiyama Kofun cluster built on the top of Mt. Ōno, 110 meters above sea level, overlooking Taketatsu Port at the northern tip of the [[Kunisaki Peninsula]]. It has a horizontal stone [[burial chamber]] which is now semi-exposed, and the interior walls of the burial chamber are decorated with line carvings on the back wall of birds, ships, and people on board, on the left wall a flock of birds, and on the right wall two birds facing each other. Since there is a depiction of a person fishing, it can be inferred that the person buried was a powerful person with deep ties to the sea, but there are many later additions and the carvings are shallow, so which lines belong to the original drawing are difficult to trace. It is estimated to have been constructed around the end of the 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
[[Grave goods]] such as iron sword fragments, iron arrowheads, sword fittings, metal rings, glass beads, and [[Sue pottery]] have unearthed. Currently, the mound has been restored and the surrounding area has been maintained as a park, with the excavated items are on display at the Kunisaki Furusato Exhibition Hall. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately 40 minutes by car from [[Usa Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

It is one of the 11 tumuli in the Nishiyama Kofun cluster built on the top of Mt. Ōno, 110 meters above sea level, overlooking Taketatsu Port at the northern tip of the [[Kunisaki Peninsula]]. It is estimated to have been constructed around the end of the 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
[[Grave goods]] such as iron sword fragments, iron arrowheads, sword fittings, metal rings, glass beads, and [[Sue pottery]] have unearthed. Currently, the mound has been restored and the surrounding area has been maintained as a park, with the excavated items are on display at the Kunisaki Furusato Exhibition Hall. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{nihongo|'''Onizuka Kofun'''|鬼塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Kunimi-cho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kunisaki, Ōita|Kunisaki, Ōita]], on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. ==Overview== The Onizuka Kofun is a slightly elliptical {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus with a diameter of 10 meters from east-to-west and 13 meters from north-to-south. It is one of the 11 tumuli in the Nishiyama Kofun cluster built on the top of Mt. Ōno, 110 meters above sea level, overlooking Taketatsu Port at the northern tip of the [[:en:Kunisaki Peninsula|Kunisaki Peninsula]]. It has a horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] which is now semi-exposed, and the interior walls of the burial chamber are decorated with line carvings on the back wall of birds, ships, and people on board, on the left wall a flock of birds, and on the right wall two birds facing each other. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] such as iron sword fragments, iron arrowheads, sword fittings, metal rings, glass beads, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] have unearthed. Currently, the mound has been restored and the surrounding area has been maintained as a park, with the excavated items are on display at the Kunisaki Furusato Exhibition Hall. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately 40 minutes by car from [[:en:Usa Station|Usa Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

It is one of the 11 tumuli in the Nishiyama Kofun cluster built on the top of Mt. Ōno, 110 meters above sea level, overlooking Taketatsu Port at the northern tip of the [[:en:Kunisaki Peninsula|Kunisaki Peninsula]]. It is estimated to have been constructed around the end of the 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Onizuka Kofun'''|鬼塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Kunimi-cho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kunisaki, Ōita|Kunisaki, Ōita]], on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211580|title=鬼塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Onizuka Kofun is a slightly elliptical {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus with a diameter of 10 meters from east-to-west and 13 meters from north-to-south. It is one of the 11 tumuli in the Nishiyama Kofun cluster built on the top of Mt. Ōno, 110 meters above sea level, overlooking Taketatsu Port at the northern tip of the [[:en:Kunisaki Peninsula|Kunisaki Peninsula]]. It has a horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] which is now semi-exposed, and the interior walls of the burial chamber are decorated with line carvings on the back wall of birds, ships, and people on board, on the left wall a flock of birds, and on the right wall two birds facing each other. Since there is a depiction of a person fishing, it can be inferred that the person buried was a powerful person with deep ties to the sea, but there are many later additions and the carvings are shallow, so which lines belong to the original drawing are difficult to trace. It is estimated to have been constructed around the end of the 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
[[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] such as iron sword fragments, iron arrowheads, sword fittings, metal rings, glass beads, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] have unearthed. Currently, the mound has been restored and the surrounding area has been maintained as a park, with the excavated items are on display at the Kunisaki Furusato Exhibition Hall. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately 40 minutes by car from [[:en:Usa Station|Usa Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Onizuka Kofun (Kunisaki)]][[ja:鬼塚古墳 (国東市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11673420]] Wikidata name: Onizuka Kofun (Kunisaki) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Onizuka Kofun | native_name =鬼塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Onizuka Kofun (Kunisaki).jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Onizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Onizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.67777778|N|131.58288889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kunisaki, Ōita|Kunisaki, Ōita]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|40|40.0|N|131|34|58.4|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Onizuka Kofun'''|鬼塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Kunimi-cho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kunisaki, Ōita|Kunisaki, Ōita]], on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211580|title=鬼塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Onizuka Kofun is a slightly elliptical {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus with a diameter of 10 meters from east-to-west and 13 meters from north-to-south. It is one of the 11 tumuli in the Nishiyama Kofun cluster built on the top of Mt. Ōno, 110 meters above sea level, overlooking Taketatsu Port at the northern tip of the [[:en:Kunisaki Peninsula|Kunisaki Peninsula]]. It has a horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] which is now semi-exposed, and the interior walls of the burial chamber are decorated with line carvings on the back wall of birds, ships, and people on board, on the left wall a flock of birds, and on the right wall two birds facing each other. Since there is a depiction of a person fishing, it can be inferred that the person buried was a powerful person with deep ties to the sea, but there are many later additions and the carvings are shallow, so which lines belong to the original drawing are difficult to trace. It is estimated to have been constructed around the end of the 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
[[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] such as iron sword fragments, iron arrowheads, sword fittings, metal rings, glass beads, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] have unearthed. Currently, the mound has been restored and the surrounding area has been maintained as a park, with the excavated items are on display at the Kunisaki Furusato Exhibition Hall. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately 40 minutes by car from [[:en:Usa Station|Usa Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

The site is approximately 40 minutes by car from [[:en:Usa Station|Usa Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{nihongo|'''Onizuka Kofun'''|鬼塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Kunimi-cho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kunisaki, Ōita|Kunisaki, Ōita]], on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211580|title=鬼塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Onizuka Kofun is a slightly elliptical {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus with a diameter of 10 meters from east-to-west and 13 meters from north-to-south. It is one of the 11 tumuli in the Nishiyama Kofun cluster built on the top of Mt. Ōno, 110 meters above sea level, overlooking Taketatsu Port at the northern tip of the [[:en:Kunisaki Peninsula|Kunisaki Peninsula]]. It has a horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] which is now semi-exposed, and the interior walls of the burial chamber are decorated with line carvings on the back wall of birds, ships, and people on board, on the left wall a flock of birds, and on the right wall two birds facing each other. Since there is a depiction of a person fishing, it can be inferred that the person buried was a powerful person with deep ties to the sea, but there are many later additions and the carvings are shallow, so which lines belong to the original drawing are difficult to trace. It is estimated to have been constructed around the end of the 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
[[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] such as iron sword fragments, iron arrowheads, sword fittings, metal rings, glass beads, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] have unearthed. Currently, the mound has been restored and the surrounding area has been maintained as a park, with the excavated items are on display at the Kunisaki Furusato Exhibition Hall. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately 40 minutes by car from [[:en:Usa Station|Usa Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Onizuka Kofun (Kunisaki)]][[ja:鬼塚古墳 (国東市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Onizuka Kofun (Kunisaki)]][[ja:鬼塚古墳 (国東市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Onizuka Kofun (Kunisaki)]][[ja:鬼塚古墳 (国東市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11673420]] Wikidata name: Onizuka Kofun (Kunisaki) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Onizuka Kofun | native_name =鬼塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Onizuka Kofun (Kunisaki).jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Onizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Onizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.67777778|N|131.58288889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kunisaki, Ōita|Kunisaki, Ōita]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|40|40.0|N|131|34|58.4|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Onizuka Kofun'''|鬼塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Kunimi-cho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kunisaki, Ōita|Kunisaki, Ōita]], on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211580|title=鬼塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Onizuka Kofun is a slightly elliptical {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus with a diameter of 10 meters from east-to-west and 13 meters from north-to-south. It is one of the 11 tumuli in the Nishiyama Kofun cluster built on the top of Mt. Ōno, 110 meters above sea level, overlooking Taketatsu Port at the northern tip of the [[:en:Kunisaki Peninsula|Kunisaki Peninsula]]. It has a horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] which is now semi-exposed, and the interior walls of the burial chamber are decorated with line carvings on the back wall of birds, ships, and people on board, on the left wall a flock of birds, and on the right wall two birds facing each other. Since there is a depiction of a person fishing, it can be inferred that the person buried was a powerful person with deep ties to the sea, but there are many later additions and the carvings are shallow, so which lines belong to the original drawing are difficult to trace. It is estimated to have been constructed around the end of the 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
[[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] such as iron sword fragments, iron arrowheads, sword fittings, metal rings, glass beads, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] have unearthed. Currently, the mound has been restored and the surrounding area has been maintained as a park, with the excavated items are on display at the Kunisaki Furusato Exhibition Hall. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately 40 minutes by car from [[:en:Usa Station|Usa Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Onizuka Kofun (Kunisaki)]][[ja:鬼塚古墳 (国東市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Onizuka Kofun (Kunisaki)]][[ja:鬼塚古墳 (国東市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Onizuka Kofun (Kunisaki)]][[ja:鬼塚古墳 (国東市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11673420]] Wikidata name: Onizuka Kofun (Kunisaki) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Onizuka Kofun | native_name =鬼塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Onizuka Kofun (Kunisaki).jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Onizuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Onizuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.67777778|N|131.58288889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kunisaki, Ōita|Kunisaki, Ōita]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|40|40.0|N|131|34|58.4|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Onizuka Kofun'''|鬼塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Kunimi-cho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kunisaki, Ōita|Kunisaki, Ōita]], on the island of [[Kyushu]] {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211580|title=鬼塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Onizuka Kofun is a slightly elliptical {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus with a diameter of 10 meters from east-to-west and 13 meters from north-to-south. It is one of the 11 tumuli in the Nishiyama Kofun cluster built on the top of Mt. Ōno, 110 meters above sea level, overlooking Taketatsu Port at the northern tip of the {{ill|Kunisaki Peninsula|en|Kunisaki Peninsula|WD=Q1792216|lt=Kunisaki Peninsula}}. It has a horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] which is now semi-exposed, and the interior walls of the burial chamber are decorated with line carvings on the back wall of birds, ships, and people on board, on the left wall a flock of birds, and on the right wall two birds facing each other. Since there is a depiction of a person fishing, it can be inferred that the person buried was a powerful person with deep ties to the sea, but there are many later additions and the carvings are shallow, so which lines belong to the original drawing are difficult to trace. It is estimated to have been constructed around the end of the 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
{{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} such as iron sword fragments, iron arrowheads, sword fittings, metal rings, glass beads, and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} have unearthed. Currently, the mound has been restored and the surrounding area has been maintained as a park, with the excavated items are on display at the Kunisaki Furusato Exhibition Hall. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately 40 minutes by car from {{ill|Usa Station|en|Usa Station|WD=Q5360282|lt=Usa Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nippō Main Line|en|Nippō Main Line|WD=Q1052288|lt=Nippō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|WD=Q6573294|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)}} *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/culturalproperties/result/4024/ Japan Heritage home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.kunisaki.oita.jp/soshiki/bunkazai/bunkazainaka.html#a01 Kuniskai City home page]{{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

{{nihongo|'''Onizuka Kofun'''|鬼塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Kunimi-cho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kunisaki, Ōita|Kunisaki, Ōita]], on the island of [[Kyushu]] {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211580|title=鬼塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Onizuka Kofun is a slightly elliptical {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus with a diameter of 10 meters from east-to-west and 13 meters from north-to-south. It is one of the 11 tumuli in the Nishiyama Kofun cluster built on the top of Mt. Ōno, 110 meters above sea level, overlooking Taketatsu Port at the northern tip of the {{ill|Kunisaki Peninsula|en|Kunisaki Peninsula|WD=Q1792216|lt=Kunisaki Peninsula}}. It has a horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] which is now semi-exposed, and the interior walls of the burial chamber are decorated with line carvings on the back wall of birds, ships, and people on board, on the left wall a flock of birds, and on the right wall two birds facing each other. Since there is a depiction of a person fishing, it can be inferred that the person buried was a powerful person with deep ties to the sea, but there are many later additions and the carvings are shallow, so which lines belong to the original drawing are difficult to trace. It is estimated to have been constructed around the end of the 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
{{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} such as iron sword fragments, iron arrowheads, sword fittings, metal rings, glass beads, and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} have unearthed. Currently, the mound has been restored and the surrounding area has been maintained as a park, with the excavated items are on display at the Kunisaki Furusato Exhibition Hall. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately 40 minutes by car from {{ill|Usa Station|en|Usa Station|WD=Q5360282|lt=Usa Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nippō Main Line|en|Nippō Main Line|WD=Q1052288|lt=Nippō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|WD=Q6573294|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)}} *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/culturalproperties/result/4024/ Japan Heritage home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.kunisaki.oita.jp/soshiki/bunkazai/bunkazainaka.html#a01 Kuniskai City home page]{{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

{{nihongo|'''Onizuka Kofun'''|鬼塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Kunimi-cho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kunisaki, Ōita|Kunisaki, Ōita]], on the island of [[Kyushu]] {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/211580|title=鬼塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Onizuka Kofun is a slightly elliptical {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus with a diameter of 10 meters from east-to-west and 13 meters from north-to-south. It is one of the 11 tumuli in the Nishiyama Kofun cluster built on the top of Mt. Ōno, 110 meters above sea level, overlooking Taketatsu Port at the northern tip of the {{ill|Kunisaki Peninsula|en|Kunisaki Peninsula|WD=Q1792216|lt=Kunisaki Peninsula|1=Kunisaki Peninsula}}. It has a horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] which is now semi-exposed, and the interior walls of the burial chamber are decorated with line carvings on the back wall of birds, ships, and people on board, on the left wall a flock of birds, and on the right wall two birds facing each other. Since there is a depiction of a person fishing, it can be inferred that the person buried was a powerful person with deep ties to the sea, but there are many later additions and the carvings are shallow, so which lines belong to the original drawing are difficult to trace. It is estimated to have been constructed around the end of the 6th century. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
{{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} such as iron sword fragments, iron arrowheads, sword fittings, metal rings, glass beads, and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} have unearthed. Currently, the mound has been restored and the surrounding area has been maintained as a park, with the excavated items are on display at the Kunisaki Furusato Exhibition Hall. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately 40 minutes by car from {{ill|Usa Station|en|Usa Station|WD=Q5360282|lt=Usa Station|1=Usa Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Nippō Main Line|en|Nippō Main Line|WD=Q1052288|lt=Nippō Main Line|1=Nippō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
